name: Raisin Scones
recipeIngredient: 6 tbsp (3/4 stick) well-chilled butter or margarine
recipeInstructions:
Makes about 25
Preheat the oven to 425°F. Lightly flour a baking sheet.
Combine the 3 1/4 cups of flour, cream of tartar, baking soda, and salt in a large bowl. Cut in the butter using a pastry blender or two knives until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Stir in the raisins and sugar. Mix in enough milk to form a soft but not sticky dough. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead gently until the dough holds together. Roll the dough out to a thickness of 1/2 inch. Cut out rounds using 2-inch cutters (push straight down; do not twist). Arrange the rounds on the prepared sheet. Dust the tops lightly with flour. Bake the scones until puffed and golden, about 10 minutes. Serve immediately with butter and jam.
